engineCodes.h2: Isuzu Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Isuzu engine — 4JJ1, 4JK1, RZ4E, 4JH1 and 4JA1 families. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Isuzu specialists.

groups.name: 4JJ1 Family – 3.0 Litre Turbo Diesel
groups.failureNote: The 4JJ1 is Isuzu’s most widely used commercial diesel, found in D-Max, MU-X and N-Series light trucks. Known for durability but susceptible to injector and DPF issues in high-mileage fleet use. Each code row includes a developer-implemented "Get Quote →" button that captures the engine code. Common failure points (expandable): 4JJ1 engines are known for injector failure (costly replacement ~£800–1,200), DPF clogging on pre-2016 models, and occasional crank pulley separation. Our rebuilt units include new injector seals, cleaned DPF or delete-compatible components, and a fully inspected fuel system .

groups.name: 4JK1 Family – 2.5 Litre Turbo Diesel
groups.failureNote: The 4JK1 is the smaller-capacity workhorse of the D-Max and Rodeo ranges. Tradespeople search for these when injector pumps or turbo failures make replacement more economical than repair. Common failure points (expandable): 4JK1 engines are known for injection pump failure (common rail issues), turbocharger carbon build-up, and EGR clogging. Our rebuilt units include a reconditioned or new Denso injection pump, cleaned intake system, and upgraded gasket sets .

groups.name: RZ4E Family – 1.9 Litre Turbo Diesel
groups.failureNote: The RZ4E is Isuzu’s modern "Blue Power" engine, designed for Euro 6 compliance. It has lower replacement volumes but owners searching specifically by this code are typically commercial operators with newer D-Max models. Common failure points (expandable): RZ4E engines have reported DPF regeneration problems and occasional AdBlue system failures in Euro 6d variants. Our rebuilt units are fully compatible with post-2018 emissions systems and include a warranty that covers DPF and injector components .

groups.name: 4JH1 / 4JA1 Families – Classic Commercial Diesels
groups.failureNote: These older diesel engines power Trooper, Rodeo and earlier D-Max models. Replacement searches are driven by cracked heads, failed injection pumps, and high-mileage wear. Common failure points (expandable): 4JH1/4JA1 engines are known for cylinder head cracking, failed glow plugs, and worn injector pumps. Our rebuilt units include pressure-tested cylinder heads, new OEM-spec glow plugs, and a full bottom-end inspection .

groups.name: 4HK1 / 4LE2 – N-Series & Industrial Engines
groups.failureNote: Fleet and commercial operators search by these codes when replacing engines in NKR, NPR and NQR trucks. These are higher-cost replacements due to engine size and commercial-duty construction. Common failure points (expandable): 4HK1/4LE2 engines commonly suffer injector failure (Denso units) and turbocharger wear in high-mileage fleet use. Our rebuilt units include new or reconditioned injectors and a balanced turbo assembly .

groups.name: 6VD1 / 6VE1 – Petrol V6
groups.failureNote: While diesel dominates Isuzu searches, the petrol V6 engines in Troopers generate consistent replacement enquiries from classic and off-road enthusiasts. Common failure points (expandable): 6VD1/6VE1 engines are known for timing belt failure (interference engine – catastrophic if snapped) and leaking rocker cover gaskets. Our rebuilt units include a new timing belt kit and full gasket set . Closing note: Not sure which engine code your Isuzu uses? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine and best quote — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Isuzu specialists.

commonProblems.h2: Common Isuzu Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Isuzu’s reputation for rugged diesel reliability is built on workhorse families like the 4JJ1, 4JK1, and RZ4E. However, UK owners – particularly D-Max tradespeople and fleet users – consistently search for solutions to DPF regeneration faults, injector failures, and coolant system weaknesses that can lead to premature replacement. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point – plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the real cost of fixing these failures versus fitting a reconditioned or rebuilt unit.

problems.h4: 4JJ1 / 4JK1 DPF & EGR System Clogging – Why The Emissions System Fails First
problems.rootCause: The DPF (diesel particulate filter) and EGR (exhaust gas recirculation) system is heavily sensitive to short trips and low-speed farm/ site work. Once soot loading exceeds passive regeneration capacity, backpressure rises, oil dilution increases, and the engine forces frequent active regens – eventually clogging the EGR cooler and intake manifold.
problems.recommendation: If your D-Max triggers limp mode with DPF/EGR codes and you have no internal knocking, a professional off-car DPF clean (£450–£750 specialist) is a cost‑effective fix. But if you’ve had multiple regens fail, oil sooting is visible, and fuel economy has dropped permanently, replacement with a fully reconditioned 4JJ1/4JK1 engine is the cheaper long‑term decision – especially for work pickups that cannot tolerate recurring downtime.
problems.cta: → Compare reconditioned 4JJ1 and 4JK1 engine prices for your D-Max

problems.h4: RZ4E 1.9L Injector & Fuel System Failure – The Common Rail Weak Spot
problems.rootCause: The Delphi common rail injectors fitted to early RZ4E engines suffer from internal needle wear and return‑leak excess. Combined with UK diesel contamination or water ingress, injector opening delay causes irregular combustion – eventually washing cylinder bores and diluting the oil sump.
problems.recommendation: On an RZ4E, any injector fault code combined with rising oil level (diesel contamination) means you are past the minor repair stage. Do not replace a single injector and hope. We recommend a full rebuilt engine with a matched, coded injector set. It costs more upfront but eliminates repeated tow‑ins and the risk of bottom‑end bearing wipeout.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt RZ4E 1.9L engines with new injector sets

problems.h4: 4JJ1 / 4JH1 Cracked Cylinder Head – Overheating Kills The Workhorse
problems.rootCause: The cylinder head between valves is prone to fine cracking after even a single overheating episode – typically a seized thermostat, failed water pump, or burst plastic end‑tank on the radiator. Once cracked, combustion gas enters coolant, the system pressurises, and the engine runs away hot.
problems.recommendation: On a high‑mileage 4JJ1 or 4JH1, a simple head swap is false economy if the rest of the engine has 140k+ hard miles. We recommend comparing prices for a complete reconditioned engine. The additional £1,400–£2,200 specialist cost buys you a fresh bottom end, upgraded gaskets, and a warranty – critical for a farm or site vehicle that cannot be reassembled twice.
problems.cta: → Compare prices for rebuilt 4JJ1 and 4JH1 complete engines

problems.h4: 4JK1 / 4JA1L Timing Gear & Oil Pump Drive Failure
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner and oil pump drive gear on these 2.5L diesels can wear prematurely due to oil viscosity breakdown (especially with extended service intervals). Chain slack alters injection timing, and oil pressure drops – eventually triggering the "low oil pressure stop engine" warning immediately before bottom‑end seizure.
problems.recommendation: If you hear chain rattle on cold start but oil pressure stays normal, fit a specialist timing chain kit (£600–£1,000). But if the red oil warning has appeared even once, do not rebuild the top end – the crank bearings are already damaged. Go straight to a replacement reconditioned engine. We see this every week on higher‑mileage 4JK1 D-Maxes used for towing.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for reconditioned 4JK1 and 4JA1L engines with new timing drive

problems.group: 4J1 Family Crankcase Breather & Oil Sludge Blockage
problems.h4: 4J1 Family Crankcase Breather & Oil Sludge Blockage
problems.affectedModels: Isuzu D-Max (all 4J1-series engines including 4JJ1, 4JK1, 4JH1), Isuzu Trooper (4J1 variants)
problems.rootCause: The factory crankcase ventilation (breather) system is undersized and clogs with oil vapour emulsion – especially on vehicles used for frequent short farm journeys that never fully heat the oil. Blocked breathers pressurise the crankcase, push oil past turbo seals, and starve the turbo + main bearings of clean oil.
problems.recommendation: For a working D-Max that idles for long periods (site power, cold starts), replace the breather system proactively every 50k miles as a £180–£280 specialist job. Once you see blue smoke on overrun or hear turbo whine, you have already ingested oil through the intake. At that point, move directly to a rebuilt engine with the upgraded breather design – it’s the only way to guarantee another 80k+ hard miles.
problems.cta: → Compare 4J1 family reconditioned engines with upgraded breather systems

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Isuzu
finalCta.paragraph: Isuzu diesel engines – particularly the 4JJ1, 4JK1, and RZ4E families – rarely fail without warning. But DPF neglect, injector wear, breather blockages, and overheating can turn a repairable engine into a complete write‑off faster than most owners expect. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Isuzu specialists who supply reconditioned, rebuilt, and used engines with upgraded components designed to eliminate the original failure points – plus nationwide delivery or supply & fit options. Stop searching forums for repair guesses. Compare real prices and get your Isuzu back on the road without the main dealer premium. All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Isuzu model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty – always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier. engineTypes.h2: Isuzu Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Isuzu engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Isuzu Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for D-Max and Rodeo owners who want factory reliability without the main dealer price premium.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £2,800 – £5,500 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Isuzu Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as uprated injector seals, reinforced cylinder heads and heavy-duty timing components – for a longer service life than a standard replacement. Ideal for high-mileage commercial vehicles.

types.title: Used Isuzu Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority for older Trooper, Rodeo or early D-Max models, with verified history and warranty available.

types.title: Isuzu Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for low-mileage D-Max, MU-X or N-Series trucks wanting a zero‑mile engine.

types.title: Isuzu Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for budget-sensitive Trooper or Rodeo models where a full recon isn't economical.

types.title: Isuzu Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Isuzu specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty – essential for D-Max owners who rely on their vehicle for daily work.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £450 – £800 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Isuzu engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Isuzu model today.

engineSizes.h2: Isuzu Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Isuzu engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Isuzu 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9-litre RZ4E turbodiesel is Isuzu’s modern, Euro‑compliant workhorse, fitted to current‑generation D‑Max pickups from 2019 onwards and the MU‑X SUV. Common rail injection and variable geometry turbo deliver a strong 163‑177bhp, but UK owner reports and specialist forums highlight blocked EGR valves and DPF regeneration problems as the primary failures driving replacement enquiries – especially on vehicles used for short, low‑speed runs . Engine code(s): RZ4E, RZ4E‑TC Compatible models (UK): D‑Max (2019–present), MU‑X (2017–present) Production years: 2017–present Common failure points: EGR valve carbon clogging, DPF blockage (short urban cycles)
items.cta: Get quotes for Isuzu 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Isuzu 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5‑litre 4JK1 family filled the mid‑range slot in D‑Max and Rodeo models from the mid‑2000s into the 2010s. These four‑cylinder diesels use common rail injection and either fixed or variable geometry turbocharging depending on the TC/TCX variant. Timing chain stretch and oil pump drive wear are the most frequently cited catastrophic failures – once the low oil pressure warning triggers, replacement is almost always more economical than rebuilding the original unit . Engine code(s): 4JK1, 4JK1‑TC, 4JK1‑TCX, 4JK1‑E5‑TC Compatible models (UK): D‑Max (2005–2016), Rodeo (2005–2012) Production years: 2005–2016 Common failure points: Timing chain stretch, oil pump drive wear, DPF clogging
items.cta: Get quotes for Isuzu 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Isuzu 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Isuzu’s 3.0‑litre 4JJ1 and earlier 4JH1 engines are legendary in the D‑Max, Rodeo and Trooper for their low‑end torque and durability – but they are not invincible. The 4JJ1 (introduced 2004) and 4JH1 (found in older D‑Max and NQR trucks) both suffer from cylinder head cracking after overheating events, as well as injector and fuel system wear. A blocked crankcase breather system can pressurise the sump and wipe out turbocharger and main bearings. Despite these weak points, a properly reconditioned 3.0‑litre Isuzu diesel remains the preferred replacement for owners who need reliable towing and payload capacity . Engine code(s): 4JJ1, 4JJ1‑TC, 4JJ1‑TCX, 4JH1, 4JH1‑TC Compatible models (UK): D‑Max (2004–2020), Rodeo (2004–2016), Trooper (2000–2006), N‑Series trucks (various) Production years: 2000–2020 Common failure points: Cracked cylinder head (overheating), injector needle wear, breather system clogging, DPF/EGR faults (later 4JJ1)
items.cta: Get quotes for Isuzu 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Isuzu 5.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 5.2‑litre four‑cylinder diesel appears in Isuzu’s heavier commercial vehicles, specifically the Forward (F‑Series) 11‑tonne rigid trucks. With 210bhp and a construction that prioritises low‑end torque over refinement, these engines are built for stop‑start urban delivery work rather than high‑speed cruising. Replacement demand is typically driven by high mileage wear rather than catastrophic design flaws, though EGR and DPF systems on Euro 4 examples can cause running issues . Engine code(s): 5.2L 4‑cylinder (specific code varies by year) Compatible models (UK): Forward F110.210 (11‑tonne rigid truck) Production years: 2008–present (commercial variants) Common failure points: High‑mileage wear, EGR/DPF clogging, injector deterioration
items.cta: Get quotes for Isuzu 5.2L diesel engines

items.title: Isuzu 7.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: Isuzu’s 7.8‑litre straight‑six diesel powers the heaviest Forward (F‑Series) rigids from 12 up to 18 tonnes gross vehicle weight, producing between 240 and 300bhp depending on tuning. These six‑cylinder units are designed for maximum durability in distribution and light construction work, using cooled EGR and common rail fuel systems to meet Euro 4 emissions standards. Replacement is generally triggered by extreme mileage, major internal wear (bearings, piston rings), or turbocharger failure rather than any single design flaw . Engine code(s): 7.8L 6‑cylinder (multiple variants) Compatible models (UK): Forward F120.240, F150.260, F180.260, F180.300 (12‑18 tonne rigid trucks) Production years: 2008–present Common failure points: Turbocharger wear, high‑mileage bottom‑end wear
items.cta: Get quotes for Isuzu 7.8L diesel engines

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Isuzu needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Isuzu specialists.

fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Isuzu engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand‑wide issues, and UK specialist supply – from the most popular diesel workhorses to heavy‑duty commercial powertrains.

items.title: Isuzu Diesel Engines
items.description: Short descriptor: Diesel is the only fuel type ever fitted to Isuzu passenger and light commercial vehicles in the UK – from the legendary 4J family in Troopers and Rodeos to the modern RZ4E and 4JJ1 engines in today’s D‑Max pickups. Common Isuzu Diesel Engine Families: RZ4E – 1.9L turbodiesel in current D‑Max, known for EGR/DPF sensitivity 4JK1 – 2.5L common‑rail diesel, timing chain stretch on higher‑mileage units 4JJ1 – 3.0L workhorse, cracked heads after overheating and breather blockages 4JH1 – older 3.0L indirect injection, injector wear and head gasket failure 4JA1 – 2.5L mechanical injection (pre‑common rail), simple but prone to oil leaks Found In: D‑Max (2004–present) – RZ4E 1.9L, 4JK1 2.5L, 4JJ1 3.0L Rodeo (1995–2012) – 4JA1 2.5L, 4JH1 3.0L, 4JJ1 3.0L (late models) Trooper (1998–2006) – 4JH1 3.0L diesel (rare 3.5L petrol not sold in UK) MU‑X (2017–present) – RZ4E 1.9L, 4JJ1 3.0L N‑Series / Forward trucks – 3.0L, 5.2L, 7.8L commercial diesels Known For (Brand‑wide): Legendary low‑end torque and towing capability across all 3.0L diesel families DPF and EGR clogging on post‑2012 models used for short urban trips 4JJ1 cylinder head cracking after even a single overheating event Breather system blockages causing sump pressurisation on 4J family engines Fuel injector wear on common‑rail systems (RZ4E, 4JK1, 4JJ1) ~95%+ of Isuzu engine replacement enquiries in the UK are diesel D‑Max related Typical Diesel Models (UK): Isuzu D‑Max 1.9L RZ4E (2019–present) Isuzu D‑Max 3.0L 4JJ1 (2004–2020) Isuzu D‑Max 2.5L 4JK1 (2005–2016) Isuzu Rodeo 3.0L 4JJ1 (2007–2012) Isuzu Trooper 3.0L 4JH1 (2000–2006) Isuzu MU‑X 3.0L 4JJ1 (2017–2022)
items.cta: Get quotes for Isuzu Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Isuzu Petrol Engines
items.description: No petrol engines were officially sold in Isuzu passenger or light commercial vehicles in the UK market. All UK‑market Rodeo, Trooper, D‑Max, MU‑X and N‑Series models are diesel‑only. (Petrol Isuzu engines exist in select international markets – e.g., the 3.5L V6 in some Rodeos – but were not UK‑supplied from factory.)

items.title: Isuzu Hybrid & Electric Powertrains
items.description: Isuzu does not currently offer hybrid or fully electric passenger vehicles or light commercials in the UK. The D‑Max, MU‑X and commercial ranges remain 100% diesel. Off‑highway electric and hybrid industrial vehicles exist but are not within the scope of this passenger/light commercial engine replacement guide.
items.cta: Compare Isuzu Hybrid & Electric Powertrains options

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Isuzu has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options – all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Isuzu specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Isuzu Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Isuzu engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Isuzu specialists.

entries.title: 4JJ1-TCX 3.0L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: Found in Isuzu D-Max (2012–2020, facelift models), MU-X (2013–2020), and some N-Series trucks. This 177 hp engine is Isuzu's most widely replaced modern diesel. Injector failure (causing bore wash and piston ring damage) and DPF clogging are the two main catastrophic failures driving replacement at 80,000–120,000 miles. A quality rebuilt unit includes new Denso injectors, upgraded injector seals, a professionally cleaned or replaced DPF, and a full bottom-end inspection.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4JJ1-TCX Isuzu engine replacement

entries.title: 4JJ1-TC 3.0L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: Powers the early D-Max (2006–2012) and first-generation MU-X. Produces 163 hp. Known for cylinder head cracking between valve seats after overheating events, plus injector seal degradation. Our rebuilt 4JJ1-TC engines include a pressure-tested or new cylinder head, uprated head gasket, and reconditioned injectors with new seals.

entries.title: 4JK1-TCY 2.5L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: The higher-output 2.5L (161 hp) found in D-Max (2012–2020). Common failures include injection pump failure (Denso common-rail pump), turbocharger carbon build-up seizing the VGT mechanism, and EGR clogging. Rebuilt units include a reconditioned or replacement Denso injection pump, cleaned turbo assembly, and new EGR cooler.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4JK1-TCY Isuzu engine replacement

entries.title: 4JK1-TC 2.5L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: The earlier 2.5L (134 hp) from D-Max (2006–2012) and Isuzu Rodeo (2006–2008). Suffers from the same injection pump and turbo failures as the TCY variant, with additional reports of timing chain stretch in high-mileage units. Our rebuilt engines include a new timing chain kit, reconditioned pump, and upgraded chain tensioner.

entries.title: RZ4E-TC 1.9L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: The "Blue Power" Euro 6 engine in D-Max (2017–present) and MU-X. Produces 148–163 hp. The most common failure is DPF regeneration problems (insufficient passive regen on short journeys), leading to oil dilution, clogged DPF, and eventual turbo damage. Some owners report AdBlue system failures on Euro 6d variants. Rebuilt units include a professionally cleaned or replacement DPF, new oil dilution management components, and full emissions system compatibility checks.
entries.cta: Get quotes for RZ4E-TC Isuzu engine replacement

entries.title: 4JH1-TC 3.0L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: Powers Isuzu Rodeo (2003–2008) and Isuzu Trooper (1998–2006 with 4JH1-TC). Produces 157 hp. Known for cylinder head cracking after overheating, failed glow plugs, and worn injector pumps. Replacement demand remains high for these older models. Our rebuilt engines include a pressure-tested cylinder head, new OEM-spec glow plugs, and a fully overhauled injection pump.

entries.title: 4JA1-L 2.5L Naturally Aspirated Diesel
entries.description: Found in Isuzu Rodeo and TF Series pickups (1990s–early 2000s). Produces approximately 85 hp. A simple, durable indirect-injection engine, but high-mileage units suffer from worn injector pumps, head gasket failure, and cracked cylinder heads. Rebuilt units include a reconditioned injection pump, new head gasket, and pressure-tested cylinder head.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4JA1-L Isuzu engine replacement

entries.title: 4JA1-T 2.5L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: The turbocharged version of the 4JA1, found in Isuzu Trooper (1998–2003) and some Rodeo models. Produces approximately 98 hp. Same failure points as the 4JA1-L, with additional turbocharger wear. Our rebuilt engines include a reconditioned turbo and upgraded oil feed pipe.

entries.title: 4HK1-TC 5.2L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: Powers Isuzu N-Series NQR and NPS trucks (2005–present). Produces 147–187 hp depending on tune. Common failures include Denso injector failure (costly eight-injector system), turbocharger wear in high-mileage fleet use, and EGR cooler blockage. Rebuilt units include new or reconditioned injectors, a balanced turbo assembly, and a cleaned EGR system.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4HK1-TC Isuzu engine replacement

entries.title: 4LE2 2.2L Turbo Diesel
entries.description: Found in Isuzu N-Series NKR light trucks and some industrial applications. Produces approximately 110 hp. Known for injector seal leaks and DPF clogging in urban delivery use. Rebuilt units include new injector seals, a cleaned DPF, and a full fuel system inspection.

entries.title: 4ZE1 2.6L Petrol (SOHC)
entries.description: Found in Isuzu Rodeo, Pickup, Trooper, and Amigo (1990s). Produces approximately 120 hp. Known for timing belt failure (interference engine – catastrophic if snapped), head gasket failure, and worn valve stem seals. Rebuilt units include a new timing belt kit, uprated head gasket, and reconditioned cylinder head.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4ZE1 Isuzu petrol engine replacement

entries.title: 6VD1 3.2L V6 Petrol
entries.description: Powers Isuzu Trooper (1997–2002) and Rodeo. Produces 202 hp. Common failures include timing belt failure (interference engine), leaking rocker cover gaskets, and worn timing belt tensioners. Our rebuilt engines include a new timing belt kit, full gasket set, and tensioner replacement.

entries.title: 6VE1 3.5L V6 Petrol
entries.description: Found in Isuzu Trooper (2002–2006), Axiom (US market), and optional on 2004 Rodeo. Produces 212–250 hp. Isuzu's first direct-injection petrol engine. Known for timing belt failure, injector clogging, and high-pressure fuel pump wear. Rebuilt units include a new timing belt kit, professionally cleaned injectors, and a reconditioned HPFP. Closing line (Block A): Can't find your engine code listed above? Use our full Isuzu engine code directory below — every code from the last four decades — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6VE1 Isuzu engine replacement

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can't find your engine code listed above? Use our full Isuzu engine code directory below — every code from the last four decades — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

directory.intro: A comprehensive alphabetical listing of every Isuzu engine code from the last 65 years. Use the search above or enter your registration for an instant match to your specific engine. All codes link to a quote request.

engineYears.h2: Isuzu Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When

years.preview: Trooper & TF Pick-Up Era: 4BD1, 4ZE1 & C223 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: Isuzu entered the UK market in 1987 with the Trooper 4x4 and TF pick-up, establishing the brand's reputation for durable diesel and petrol engines sourced from its Japanese commercial vehicle division. Key Changes Isuzu Trooper launched in UK with 2.2-litre C223-T diesel and 2.0-litre petrol as entry options 2.8-litre 4JB1-T diesel replaces C223 unit on Trooper from late 1980s - significant power and torque step-up 4ZE1 2.6-litre petrol introduced for Trooper petrol variants - SOHC eight-valve unit sourced from Isuzu's car division TF pick-up range shares 4JB1-T diesel architecture - Vauxhall Brava badged variant uses same engine IBC Vehicles (GM-Isuzu UK joint venture) supports UK production and parts supply Main Engines C223-T - 2.2 diesel (Trooper 2.2 D - early models) 4JB1-T - 2.8 turbo diesel (Trooper 2.8 TDI, Vauxhall Brava 2.5 TD) 4JB1-TC - 2.8 turbo diesel intercooled (Trooper late 1990s enhanced) 4ZE1 - 2.6 SOHC petrol (Trooper 2.6 petrol) 4FC1T - 1.6 turbo petrol (early Isuzu van-derived applications) Popular Models Isuzu Trooper (UBS) Isuzu TF Pick-Up Vauxhall Brava (Isuzu-derived) Known For 4JB1-T is one of the most mechanically robust diesel units ever fitted to a UK 4x4 - proven to well beyond 250,000 miles with basic servicing across farming and off-road communities C223-T pre-dates common-rail technology - indirect injection design is forgiving of contaminated fuel but lacks the power output of later Isuzu diesels 4ZE1 petrol is torquey and reliable but fuel consumption on UK long-distance and towing use is a significant drawback - most owners preferred the diesel Vauxhall Brava badging masks Isuzu mechanical identity - parts are fully interchangeable but sourcing requires knowing the Isuzu engine code [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: C223-T, 4JB1-T, 4JB1-TC, 4ZE1, 4FC1T
years.cta: Search for 1987–1997 Isuzu Trooper replacement engines from UK 4x4 specialists

years.preview: Trooper 3.0 4JX1 Introduction & Monterey Diesel 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The 3.0-litre 4JX1 direct injection diesel replaced the 4JB1 across the refreshed Trooper range from 1998, delivering improved power and refinement - but it arrived with one of the most documented injector failure patterns in the brand's UK history. Key Changes 4JX1 3.0-litre DOHC diesel introduced in refreshed Trooper from 1998 - common-rail direct injection architecture replaces IDI unit 4JG2TC V6 3.2-litre petrol offered in Trooper as premium alternative to diesel Monterey 3.0 diesel (4JX1) offered alongside Trooper as alternative bodystyle in UK 4JX1 injector design flaw becomes apparent from early UK ownership - fuel dilution into oil sump leads to head gasket failure on neglected examples Euro 2 and early Euro 3 compliance across the range Main Engines 4JX1 - 3.0 DOHC common-rail diesel (Trooper 3.0 DT, Monterey 3.0 DT) 4JG2TC - 3.2 V6 DOHC petrol (Trooper 3.2 V6 petrol) 4JB1-TC - 2.8 turbo diesel (continued on entry Trooper) Popular Models Isuzu Trooper (UBS - 1998 refresh) Isuzu Monterey 3.0 Known For 4JX1 injector O-ring seals are the defining fault of this generation - fuel leaks into the oil sump cause oil dilution, and if undetected lead to head gasket failure or catastrophic engine seizure UK specialist community recommends preventative injector replacement at 60,000 miles on all 4JX1 examples - some UK rebuilders substitute the entire engine for a 4JB1-T unit as a permanent fix 4JG2TC V6 petrol is smooth and powerful but running costs on UK fuel prices deterred most buyers - engine itself is mechanically sound with few reported failures 4JX1 can be a strong, long-lasting unit when injector seals are proactively maintained - owner communities emphasise that neglect is the primary cause of failure, not inherent design weakness [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 4JX1, 4JG2TC, 4JB1-TC
years.cta: Find 1998–2002 Isuzu Trooper 4JX1 replacement engines from UK 4x4 engine specialists

years.preview: Rodeo Launch, 4JH1 Diesel & Final Trooper Year 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The Isuzu Rodeo pick-up replaced the ageing TF in the UK from 2004, introducing the 4JH1-TC 3.0-litre diesel as the brand's primary workhorse engine - and Isuzu became the first manufacturer to offer a diesel automatic pick-up in the UK. Key Changes Isuzu Rodeo launched in UK (2004) with 4JH1-TC 3.0-litre turbo diesel - 130 PS, 360 Nm, 3-tonne towing Rodeo 3.0 diesel offered as first-ever diesel automatic pick-up in the UK market - ZF 4-speed auto gearbox Trooper production continues into 2003–2004 final run - 4JX1 diesel and 4JG2TC V6 petrol remain unchanged 4JH1 replaces the earlier 4JB1 architecture in the commercial pick-up range - improved torque curve for agricultural and trade use Euro 3 compliance maintained across pick-up range Main Engines 4JH1-TC - 3.0 four-cylinder turbo diesel (Isuzu Rodeo 3.0, Vauxhall Brava final - shared architecture) 4JX1 - 3.0 DOHC diesel (Trooper final production run) 4JG2TC - 3.2 V6 petrol (Trooper - final year) Popular Models Isuzu Rodeo (TFR - 2004 onward) Isuzu Trooper (final production - 2003) Known For 4JH1-TC is a torque-biased, mechanically straightforward diesel - agricultural and trade communities regard it as one of the most reliable units in its class for genuine work use Turbocharger wear on high-mileage 4JH1 examples is the leading failure point - variable-geometry turbos on later variants are more susceptible than fixed-vane earlier units Rodeo MOT pass rates average 74% - suspension and braking components are the primary concerns rather than engine reliability per se Trooper exits UK market with the 4JX1's reputation still mixed - final-year examples bought new and properly maintained have proven long-lived [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 4JH1-TC, 4JX1, 4JG2TC
years.cta: Compare 2003–2006 Isuzu Rodeo and Trooper replacement engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: Rodeo D-Max First Gen, 4JK1 & 4JJ1 Introduction 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The Isuzu D-Max nameplate arrived in the UK as a rebadged Rodeo from 2007, and by 2011 the brand had fully transitioned to the new 4JJ1 3.0-litre DOHC diesel - a unit that would define Isuzu's reputation in the UK pick-up market for the following decade. Key Changes Isuzu D-Max launched as a rebadge and upgrade of the Rodeo platform from 2007 in UK 4JJ1-TC 3.0-litre DOHC turbo diesel introduced - common-rail, variable geometry turbo, significantly more refined than 4JH1 4JK1-TC 2.5-litre DOHC diesel offered as entry-level engine across D-Max range in some markets - 3.0 4JJ1 dominant in UK Euro 4 compliance from 2008 - 4JJ1 calibrated for UK fuel quality and drive cycles D-Max positioned as direct competitor to Toyota Hilux and Mitsubishi L200 in UK commercial and lifestyle pick-up segment Main Engines 4JJ1-TC - 3.0 DOHC four-cylinder common-rail turbo diesel (D-Max 3.0, multiple trim levels) 4JJ1-TCX - 3.0 DOHC enhanced output variant (D-Max high-output spec - 163 PS) 4JK1-TC - 2.5 DOHC four-cylinder turbo diesel (entry-level D-Max - limited UK fitment) 4JH1-TC - 3.0 diesel (Rodeo continued in parallel early years) Popular Models Isuzu D-Max (TF - first generation UK) Isuzu Rodeo (outgoing parallel production) Known For 4JJ1-TC is a significant step forward from the 4JH1 - improved refinement, stronger power delivery, and competitive fuel economy for a 3.0-litre diesel in a workhorse pick-up EGR valve fouling is a documented issue on 4JJ1 units used predominantly for short-journey or low-load driving - agricultural use with high-load driving cycles generally keeps the EGR clean Valve clearances on the 4JJ1 are manual adjustment - most owners are unaware this is a scheduled maintenance requirement; neglect leads to a characteristic ticking noise on cold start Timing chain design (rather than belt) on 4JJ1 is a positive differentiator - no scheduled belt replacement cost, and chain longevity is strong when oil change intervals are respected [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 4JJ1-TC, 4JJ1-TCX, 4JK1-TC, 4JH1-TC
years.cta: Find 2007–2011 Isuzu D-Max 4JJ1 replacement engines from UK pick-up engine specialists

years.preview: D-Max Second Generation, 4JJ1 Maturity & Euro 5 🔴 Major Reliability Revision
years.description: Short intro: The second-generation Isuzu D-Max arrived in 2012 with a revised 4JJ1 engine, significantly improved interior, and expanded model range - establishing the D-Max as the UK's most reliable pick-up in multiple owner surveys. Key Changes Second-generation D-Max (2012) launched with revised 4JJ1 engine - updated injectors, improved NVH, and Euro 5 compliance 4JJ1 output standardised at 163 PS / 380 Nm across UK range - consistent specification simplifies fleet and trade procurement DPF fitted to 4JJ1 from Euro 5 compliance onward - diesel particulate filter on exhaust system D-Max quickly becomes dominant force in UK pick-up sales - outsells rivals in several fleet categories No petrol engine options - Isuzu commits UK range entirely to diesel Main Engines 4JJ1-TCX Euro 5 - 3.0 DOHC common-rail diesel with DPF (D-Max 3.0 - all variants 2012 onward) 4JJ1-TC - 3.0 earlier spec (D-Max fleet and entry variants - early production) Popular Models Isuzu D-Max (TFR - second generation) Isuzu D-Max Blade Isuzu D-Max Utah Isuzu D-Max Yukon Known For Second-generation 4JJ1 builds strongly on the first - injector contamination from poor-quality UK diesel is the most common service issue, manifesting as rough idle and black smoke DPF blockage is the leading warranty and independent repair enquiry for the 2012–2016 D-Max - short-journey and urban driving patterns create regeneration failure; a motorway blast at speed typically resolves early-stage blockage 4JJ1 timing chain, block, and head integrity are genuinely strong - engine replacement is rarely needed below 200,000 miles when DPF and EGR are properly maintained D-Max achieves best-in-class reliability rankings in multiple UK fleet surveys during this period - Isuzu's reputation for commercial durability fully established [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 4JJ1-TCX (Euro 5), 4JJ1-TC
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012–2016 Isuzu D-Max 4JJ1 replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: RZ4E 1.9 Introduction, Euro 6 Compliance & D-Max Dual-Engine Era 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: Isuzu introduced the all-new RZ4E 1.9-litre turbo diesel in 2017 as the most significant powertrain change in the D-Max's UK history - a lighter, cleaner unit designed for Euro 6 compliance, running alongside the revised 4JJ1 3.0-litre in a dual-engine range. Key Changes RZ4E-TC 1.9-litre four-cylinder turbo diesel launched in D-Max (2017) - 164 PS, 360 Nm, Euro 6 compliant RZ4E is 20% lighter than the 4JJ1, improves fuel efficiency by approximately 19% - up to 39.4 mpg combined on official cycle 4JJ1 3.0-litre retained as flagship engine for maximum towing - D-Max becomes a dual-engine range Euro 6 emissions hardware includes DPF and SCR (AdBlue) on both engine variants RZ4E architecture draws on Isuzu's commercial vehicle engine division - designed for longevity under sustained load Main Engines RZ4E-TC - 1.9 four-cylinder turbo diesel Euro 6 (D-Max 1.9 - entry and mid-range trims) 4JJ1-TCX Euro 6 - 3.0 DOHC diesel (D-Max 3.0 - upper range and fleet-towing spec) RZ4E-MID - 1.9 diesel (specific mid-output calibration for D-Max fleet spec variants) Popular Models Isuzu D-Max (2017 facelift - all trims) Isuzu D-Max Blade Isuzu D-Max Arctic Trucks AT35 Isuzu D-Max Utah Known For RZ4E 1.9 delivers genuinely improved fuel economy and cleaner emissions versus the 4JJ1 - competitive against Mitsubishi and Ford Ranger diesel units for real-world fuel return Euro 6 AdBlue and SCR system on both RZ4E and 4JJ1 introduces additional maintenance obligations - NOx sensor failures and AdBlue dosing faults are emerging fault patterns on higher-mileage post-2017 examples 4JJ1 3.0-litre in Euro 6 spec retains its core mechanical robustness - EGR valve fouling continues to be the primary maintenance-related fault, especially for urban fleet use D-Max Arctic Trucks AT35 with 1.9 RZ4E generates strong UK enthusiast demand - engine reliability in this application is well-regarded [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: RZ4E-TC, RZ4E-MID, 4JJ1-TCX (Euro 6)
years.cta: Find 2017–2020 Isuzu D-Max RZ4E and 4JJ1 Euro 6 replacement engines from verified UK suppliers

years.preview: Third Generation D-Max, 4JJ3 3.0 & RZ4E Refinement 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The third-generation Isuzu D-Max arrived in 2021 with the all-new 4JJ3 3.0-litre diesel replacing the long-running 4JJ1 at the top of the range, delivering 190 PS and 450 Nm - the most powerful and torque-rich engine Isuzu has offered in the UK pick-up market. Key Changes 4JJ3 3.0-litre DOHC diesel introduced in third-generation D-Max (2021) - new unit in same displacement class as 4JJ1 but substantially re-engineered 4JJ3 outputs 190 PS and 450 Nm - significant increase over 4JJ1's 163 PS / 380 Nm Variable geometry turbocharger and high-pressure common-rail injection system on 4JJ3 - Euro 6d compliant RZ4E 1.9-litre refined and retained for entry and mid D-Max variants - Euro 6d spec D-Max MU-X SUV launched in select UK markets alongside the pick-up - same 4JJ3 architecture Main Engines 4JJ3 - 3.0 DOHC VGT common-rail diesel 190 PS / 450 Nm (D-Max 3.0 - all upper-range and 4x4 variants) RZ4E-MID - 1.9 turbo diesel 164 PS / 360 Nm (D-Max 1.9 - entry, HR, and 4x2 variants) 4JJ3 (auto spec) - 3.0 diesel with 6-speed automatic (D-Max LSE 4x4 AT) Popular Models Isuzu D-Max (third generation - all trims) Isuzu D-Max V-Cross 4x4 Isuzu D-Max Arctic Trucks AT35 Isuzu D-Max Blade Extended Cab Isuzu MU-X (select UK availability) Known For 4JJ3 core mechanical reliability is strong - cast iron block, aluminium head, timing chain, and DOHC architecture reflects Isuzu's commercial engine heritage rather than a cost-reduced passenger car unit EGR valve and DPF management remain the primary failure points on 4JJ3 in short-trip or urban driving - long-distance and farm/trade duty cycles produce far fewer issues Valve clearance adjustment is still a manual process on 4JJ3 - this remains a maintenance task that many independent garages overlook, making specialist Isuzu servicing important 4JJ3 estimated engine lifespan of 400,000 km under correct maintenance is widely cited by Isuzu specialists - positioning it among the most durable light commercial diesels available in the UK pick-up sector [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 4JJ3, RZ4E-MID, RZ4E-TC (Euro 6d) Not sure which year your Isuzu was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Isuzu specialists.
years.cta: Compare 2021–2025 Isuzu D-Max 4JJ3 and RZ4E replacement engine quotes from UK specialists

faq.h2: Isuzu Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Isuzu owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Isuzu engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Isuzu D-Max 3.0L 4JJ1 engine typically costs £2,500 – £4,000 supplied only, while a 1.9L RZ4E ranges from £2,200 – £3,500. Adding supply and fitting adds £500 – £900 depending on your location and garage rates. Used engines start from £1,200 – £2,000 but carry higher risk and shorter warranties. Always compare three quotes – price variations of £1,000+ on identical engine codes are common across UK specialists. → Compare Isuzu engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Isuzu engine replacement?
items.answer: Four key factors: engine size and code (3.0L 4JJ1 costs more than 1.9L RZ4E), condition (rebuilt with upgrades costs more than basic reconditioned), model age (older Rodeo and Trooper engines are rarer and more expensive), and whether you choose supply-only or supply-and-fit. Labour costs vary significantly – London specialists typically charge 20-30% more than Midlands or Northern workshops. Our comparison tool shows you exact fitted prices from multiple Isuzu specialists, so you can see exactly where savings are possible. → Get quotes from UK Isuzu engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Isuzu?
items.answer: Yes, in most cases – especially if the chassis, gearbox, and body are solid. A professionally reconditioned 4JJ1 or 4JK1 engine with upgraded pistons and timing components will often outlast the original, giving another 100,000+ miles. The alternative – selling your D-Max as a non-runner and buying a replacement vehicle – typically costs £8,000+ more than an engine replacement. For Isuzu pickups that hold their value well (2015+ D-Max models), replacement is almost always the financially sensible decision. → Compare rebuilt vs used Isuzu engine prices
items.cta: Get a Isuzu engine quote now

items.question: What's included in a Isuzu engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply-and-fit package includes: sourcing the correct reconditioned or used engine, removal of your failed unit, installation of the replacement, new oil, filters, coolant, and gaskets, a road test to confirm correct operation, and workmanship warranty (typically 6-12 months). The old engine is disposed of or returned according to your preference. Fitting costs range from £500 – £900 on a D-Max and £600 – £1,100 on older Rodeo or Trooper models. Always confirm whether collection/delivery and VAT are included in the quoted price. → Find Isuzu engine supply and fit deals
items.cta: Get a Isuzu engine quote now

items.question: How long does a Isuzu engine replacement take?
items.answer: A straightforward like-for-like swap on a D-Max typically takes 3–5 working days. More complex replacements – older Rodeo or Trooper engines where parts availability is limited – can take 5–7 days. The biggest variable is engine availability: our network stocks commonly replaced codes like the 4JJ1 and 4JK1, but rarer units may need 5-10 days to source. Most specialists will provide a courtesy vehicle or can arrange recovery and delivery to minimise downtime. Ask each supplier for their current lead time when you request quotes. → Compare Isuzu engine replacement times and prices
items.cta: Get a Isuzu engine quote now

items.question: Do reconditioned Isuzu engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes – every reconditioned, rebuilt, or remanufactured Isuzu engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Most specialists offer extended 24‑month coverage on premium rebuilt units that include upgraded components (e.g., timing chains, pistons, bearings). The warranty typically covers major internal parts: crankshaft, conrods, pistons, cylinder head, camshaft, timing chain, oil pump, and gaskets. Always confirm the exact terms with your chosen supplier – some warranties exclude labour costs or require the fitting to be done by an approved garage. → Find reconditioned Isuzu engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Isuzu engine?
items.answer: A used engine is cheaper upfront (£1,200 – £2,000) and makes sense if you're selling the vehicle soon or need a temporary fix. However, it comes with the original failure risks – a used 4JK1 may still have a stretched timing chain, and a used 4JJ1 could develop injector or breather issues. A reconditioned engine costs more (£2,200 – £4,000) but includes new piston rings, bearings, gaskets, seals, and a full warranty. If your Isuzu has a known problem engine family (e.g., 4JK1 timing chain, 4JJ1 cracked head potential), a reconditioned unit with upgraded parts is strongly recommended for long-term ownership. → Compare used vs recon Isuzu engine costs side by side
items.cta: Compare used and reconditioned Isuzu engine options

items.question: Where can I find Isuzu engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of vetted Isuzu engine specialists, reconditioners, and fitting centres. We have strong coverage across the Midlands, North West (Manchester/Liverpool), Yorkshire, and Scotland where D-Max and Rodeo ownership density is highest. Simply enter your registration number – our system instantly matches you with suppliers who stock your specific Isuzu engine code (e.g., 4JJ1, 4JK1, RZ4E, 4JH1) and offer fitting at garages within 25 miles of your postcode. Nationwide delivery is also available for supply-only purchases. → Find Isuzu engine specialists near your postcode
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Isuzu engine specialists

items.question: What are the most common Isuzu engine failures?
items.answer: The most common failures driving Isuzu replacement enquiries are: 4JJ1 and 4JH1 cracked cylinder heads (following overheating – replacement is the only permanent fix), 4JK1 timing chain stretch and oil pump drive failure (low oil pressure warning means bottom-end damage), RZ4E injector failure and bore wash (diesel contamination in oil sump), and 4J family crankcase breather blockages (pressurises sump, wipes turbo and bearings). DPF/EGR clogging on post-2012 D-Max models is also a frequent reason owners choose replacement over repeated repairs. These failures typically give little warning and, once diagnosed, replacement with a reconditioned unit is more cost-effective than attempting a partial repair. → Get quotes for reconditioned Isuzu engines – fixed price, no hidden costs
items.cta: Get a Isuzu engine quote now

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Isuzu engine?
items.answer: Compare at least three quotes – prices for the same engine code (e.g., 4JJ1) can vary by £1,000+ between suppliers. Consider used (£1,200–£2,000) if your budget is tight or you're selling soon. For long-term ownership, a reconditioned engine with upgraded components (£2,500–£4,000 with warranty) is better value. Ask whether the quoted price includes fitting, delivery, VAT, and disposal of your old engine. Supply-only is cheaper if you have a trusted local garage. Finally, be flexible on location – driving an extra 50 miles to a Midlands specialist can save £300+ compared to a London workshop. Use our free comparison tool to see all prices in one place before you commit. → Use our free comparison tool to get the best Isuzu engine price today
